LanguageData.OutputModTip: The quests, maps, and dialogs generated by CQF will be saved in the output Mod
LanguageData.RuleTip:
Rules use method: The format of a rule is: key->text. Text can be specified using the [key] to indicate that the text here is the text that the key refers to. If the key has multiple corresponding texts, a random one will be selected during generation. When editing quest text, you can also use [the key of the object] to obtain the corresponding text of the object. The rules are applied in different places, requiring different keys to output the final text. For example, when rules are used for quest names, questName is required to output the final text. For more details, please refer to examples provided in this mod.
Here are some key requirements:
QuestName: questName-> QuestDescription: questDescription-> SiteName: SiteName->
LanguageData.DesignatorDestroyThingDesc: Destroy all things within the selected area.
LanguageData.DestroyAllPawn: Destroy all pawns on current map
LanguageData.DestroyAllFilth: Destroy all filth in current map
LanguageData.CQF_PawnGroupMaker_Tip: Generate pawn based on pawn group maker of faction
LanguageData.PawnDataFactionTip: If this is random hostile/neutral/ally,it will randomly select random hostile/neutral/ally faction to generate during generation, and if it is map faction,it will select the faction of the current map to generate
LanguageData.SpawnTypeTip_BuildingTick: When generating, it will automatically select the building in the generating location as the "generator", and spawn pawns at intervals when the generator exists, but it will be invalidated when generator is destroyed.
LanguageData.PawnDataNameTip:
Used for outputting signals. Each pawn will output a tag when executing events such as being killed or injured [DataName] [Number] The signal of behavior (in fact, the relevant task number is added at the beginning to identify which task needs to receive the signal). The number is assigned according to the generation order when generating all entities of this data, starting from 0. For example, if a total of ten entities are generated, the fifth generated number is 4 If a map is generated with a data name of "HaiLuan", the fifth spawned pawn will emit a signal of "HaiLuan.4.Killed" when she is killed, When spawned from the LootBox, [label] is the name of the loot box.If not, it means none and will not have a '.' after the label.
The following is a list of behaviors and signals corresponding to each other: ChangeFaction Change faction and the new faction is not a player faction: ChangedFactionToNonPlayer Change faction and the new faction is a player faction: ChangedFactionToPlayer When generating pawn: Spawned When the pawn is destroyed: Despawned When an pawn is killed: Killed Suffer damage from player faction: TookDamageFromPlayer Leaving Map: LeftMap
When spawning, the spawned pawn will be recorded in the "quest database", and its "key" is its data name
LanguageData.CantReseverveOrReachLootBox: Can't reseverve or reach loot box
LanguageData.UseLootDef: Use loot def (In order to save loading time)
LanguageData.CQFStuffTip: If the stuff is null and the generated requires a null, a null suitable for the generated will be randomly generated as its stuff
LanguageData.UnableToInterviewTargetIsHostile: Unable to interview, target is hostile
LanguageData.Designator_TerrainAndRoofDesc: Set a roof or terrain within the specified range.
LanguageData.ReplaceDefTip: When generating the map, a replacement data is randomly extracted from list, and things or terrain are replaced according to the extracted data when generating them.
LanguageData.MapDataFactionTip: When generating the map, if it generates buildings without factions, set factions for the buildings based on this
LanguageData.CQF_MapGenerator_Tip: If there are no special circumstances, please use the default CQF generator
LanguageData.MapDefWithChance_Tip: When generating, a generation pool containing map def and their chance will be generated, and one will be extracted based on the chance of each def for generation.
LanguageData.TagWithChance_Tip: Search for all custom map def during generation, and add them to the generation pool if they contain corresponding tags. The chance of all def containing the same tag is the same.
LanguageData.QE_Designator_MoveInDesc: Mark items that need to be moved into the sub map, pay attention! It can only be used when there is a target sub map.
LanguageData.QE_Designator_MoveOutDesc: Mark items that need to be moved out from the sub map, pay attention! Only when the target map is a sub map of a certain map can it be used.
LanguageData.QE_Designator_MoveToRoot: Move out to the starting point
LanguageData.ThereIsntTargetSubMap: The current map does not have {0}
LanguageData.TargetIsntWithinSubMap: The target is not within the sub map
LanguageData.CustomTrapTip: It is recommended to destroy the trap after triggering to avoid an infinite explosion caused by a combo with explosive action.
LanguageData.SignalOnlyIsValidInPart: Signal is valid only in the part:
LanguageData.SignalOnlyIsValidInPartTip: When the zone core generates a map part, enabling this option prevents the signal from being effective on things outside the map part.
LanguageData.OpeningActionsTip: The actions executed when opened, with the target key: "Inner" being an internal thing
LanguageData.CQFSetFactionDesc: Set the faction for selected things.
LanguageData.CQFSetStyleDesc: Set the style for selected things.
LanguageData.CQFSpawnThingTip: Tip: Right click to open the menu and select the thing to generate.
LanguageData.ExtraKindsTip: The pawn kinds that will be generated together during generation. They will use same faction, hediffs, and other parameters from pawn data.
LanguageData.CQFPasteDesc: Paste the copied data into a selected thing.
LanguageData.CustomMapIsPartTip: It is a part of a complete map that will be extracted and generated when randomly generating regions.
LanguageData.Designator_RoundDesc: Select a circular zone to save as a custom map def.(Only applicable to saving map part. If saving a whole map, please use Save Whole Map)
LanguageData.Designator_RectangleDesc: Select a rectangle zone to save as a custom map def.(Only applicable to saving map part. If saving a whole map, please use Save Whole Map)
LanguageData.Designator_RectangleUseCentreDesc: Select a radius centered on a point and save the area as a custom map def.(Only applicable to saving map part. If saving a whole map, please use Save Whole Map)
LanguageData.OutOfVanillaRange: Exceeding the range of the vanilla, with a maximum of{0}
LanguageData.CoreZoneRotationTip: It is the direction of the core relative to the centre of the zone.
LanguageData.MapPartGenerationLimit_Tip: When the zone core is generating, it will increase the counter corresponding to the generation key by one, and once it reaches the upper limit, it willn't generate part.
LanguageData.ReserveGenerationThingTip: When the Zone core cannot be generated, it will generate reserved thing in place, such as creating a wall to block this road.
LanguageData.ReserveGenerationThing_MapDef_Tip: After the map is generated, reserved thing are generated in all open spaces that have not undergone "zone generation". Zone generation refers to the generation of map parts by the "zone core".
LanguageData.QE_Designator_Disgenerate_Desc: Mark the area, and the zone core will not generate the "map part" in the disgenerate area.
LanguageData.QE_Designator_Disdestroy_Desc: Mark the area, and when generating custom map data, the thing within the area will not be destroyed.
LanguageData.GenerationLimitTip: If it is 0,it will be ignored.
LanguageData.replaceMapGeneration_Tip: Only custom maps are generated when generating, not field environments like the vanilla will generate
LanguageData.CQF_CancelLandfillDesc: Cancel the landfill operation for this building
LanguageData.CQF_StartLandfillDesc: Mark this building to be landfilled. WARNING: This will destroy the building's attached map!
LanguageData.ToggleOutputModeDesc: Switch to output mode, transmitting power to the receiver
LanguageData.CQF_SetReceiverDesc: Set the receiver. The receiver can be a receiving building on the parent map or any child map of the current map.
